explanation: The contract reads msg.value inside a loop. msg.value is fixed for the whole transaction, but code that uses it per iteration typically treats the single payment as if it arrived once per pass (crediting or forwarding msg.value N times while the ether was only received once). This is especially dangerous in delegatecall based multicall patterns, where one msg.value can be reused across many batched calls.
impact: An attacker pays once and gets credited or paid out many times, draining the contract of other users' ether; the Opyn ETH put exploit and several multicall bugs used exactly this pattern.
